title: relative-strength output
description: Canonical structured output for the relative-strength skill. Layer 1 (the data); Layer 2 (rendered table) is described in references/rendering.md.

as_of.description: Local 'as-of' date the run was anchored to.

fetched_at.description: UTC timestamp when the script ran.

watchlist.description: Tickers the caller passed via --watchlist (uppercased, dedup'd).

benchmark.description: Benchmark ticker the watchlist is compared against. Default SPY.

windows_days.description: Lookback windows in trading days, sorted ascending.

include_sectors.description: Whether the 11 SPDR sector ETFs were included in the ranking.

n_tickers.description: Total number of tickers ranked (watchlist + sectors if include_sectors).

results.description: One entry per ranked ticker. Sorted by composite_rs_percentile descending; nulls last.

rs_by_window.description: Relative strength in basis points per window. Keys are '{window}d_bps' (e.g. '5d_bps'). Null when the ticker or benchmark lacks history for that window.

return_by_window.description: Total return in decimal per window. Keys are '{window}d_pct' (e.g. '5d_pct'). Null when history is insufficient.

composite_rs_percentile.description: Mean of within-watchlist percentile ranks across the windows that produced a non-null RS. Range 0-100. Null when no window had enough history. Rewards consistency: a name in the top quartile every window scores higher than a name that's #1 once and last otherwise.

trend_label.description: improving: short-window RS strictly greater than long-window RS (recent acceleration). deteriorating: short < long (recent decay). stable_leader: every window positive. stable_laggard: every window negative. mixed: anything else.

n_obs_per_window.description: Number of trading-day observations available per window. Keys are '{window}d' (e.g. '5d').

leaders_top_3.description: Top 3 tickers by composite RS percentile, descending.

laggards_bottom_3.description: Bottom 3 tickers by composite RS percentile, worst first.

tier_caveats.description: Human-readable caveats: insufficient history, sector overlay note, and the always-on 'not predictive on its own' line.

sources.description: All Massive endpoints called during the run, with fetched-at timestamps for audit.
